headlines.title: Russia's Fuel Crisis Deepens as Putin Acknowledges Growing Gasoline Shortages

headlines.title: Pakistan Plans to Boost LPG Imports and Mulls Cheaper Oil Supply from Iran
headlines.summary: Pakistan will raise its imports of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) from Iran and considers the idea of importing cheaper crude oil from the Islamic Republic, Pakistani Minister of Petroleum, Ali Pervaiz Malik, has said. headlines.title: Gulf Producers Race to Load Oil and LNG as Hormuz Stays Open
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, and Qatar continue loading oil and LNG onto vessels at their Persian Gulf ports despite the attacks on two commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz and the weekend tensions between the U.S. and Iran. headlines.title: Pakistan LNG Seeks Emergency Cargo After Weekend Hormuz Flare-Up
headlines.summary: Pakistan’s state LNG importer is urgently seeking to procure an LNG cargo for delivery this week, with offers due on June 29, as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains volatile amid persistent threats to tankers and reignited U.S.-Iran tensions. headlines.title: Oil Prices Climb as U.S.-Iran Flare-Up Shakes Market Complacency
headlines.summary: Oil prices were climbing early on Monday morning after a fresh escalation between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend.
